title: Verify VFree 60+ device appears in Inventory after onboarding
steps: Navigate to the Inventory section of the application.
Locate the search or filter field.
Enter 'VFree 60+' as the search term.
Verify that the VFree 60+ device is listed in the Inventory results.
Record the device ID or serial number of the VFree 60+ device.
expected: The VFree 60+ device is visible in the Inventory list with its device details (ID, serial number, status) displayed.

title: Verify VFree 60+ device is absent from Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list
steps: Navigate to Manage -> Policies section.
Click on 'Individual Device' tab or sub-section.
Locate the search or filter field within the Individual Device list.
Enter the device ID or serial number of the VFree 60+ device (previously recorded from Inventory).
Press Enter or click Search.
Observe the search results.
expected: The VFree 60+ device is NOT displayed in the Individual Device list under Manage -> Policies, despite being present in Inventory.

title: Verify policy removal is blocked when VFree 60+ device is missing from Individual Device list
steps: Navigate to Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list.
Attempt to locate the VFree 60+ device using its device ID or serial number.
Confirm the device is not listed in the Individual Device list.
Attempt to perform a policy removal action (e.g., click 'Remove Policy' or similar button) for the VFree 60+ device.
Observe the system response.
expected: The system does not allow policy removal for the VFree 60+ device because it is not present in the Individual Device list. An appropriate error message or disabled action is displayed.

title: Verify device visibility consistency between Inventory and Manage -> Policies for VFree 60+
steps: Navigate to the Inventory section.
Search for 'VFree 60+' and confirm the device is listed.
Record the exact device name, ID, and status from Inventory.
Navigate to Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list.
Search for the same device using the recorded ID or name.
Compare the visibility status of the device between the two sections.
expected: The VFree 60+ device is present in Inventory but absent from the Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list, indicating an inconsistency in device visibility across sections.

title: Verify other device models appear correctly in both Inventory and Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list
steps: Navigate to the Inventory section.
Select a device model that is NOT VFree 60+ (e.g., a standard device model).
Record its device ID or serial number.
Navigate to Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list.
Search for the non-VFree 60+ device using its recorded ID or serial number.
Verify the device appears in the Individual Device list.
Attempt to remove a policy from this device.
Observe the result.
expected: The non-VFree 60+ device appears in both Inventory and Manage -> Policies -> Individual Device list, and policy removal can be performed successfully on this device.

title: Verify policy assignment from Inventory for VFree 60+ device
steps: Navigate to the Inventory section.
Search for and select the VFree 60+ device.
Look for a 'Assign Policy' or 'Manage Policy' option in the device details or action menu.
Attempt to assign a policy to the VFree 60+ device from Inventory.
Observe the system response and any error messages.
expected: The system either successfully assigns the policy from Inventory, or displays an appropriate message indicating that policy management must be done from Manage -> Policies section. If the latter, verify that the device is missing from that section.

title: Verify policy removal from Inventory for VFree 60+ device
steps: Navigate to the Inventory section.
Search for and select the VFree 60+ device.
Look for a 'Remove Policy' or 'Unassign Policy' option in the device details or action menu.
Attempt to remove the policy from the VFree 60+ device directly from Inventory.
Observe the system response and any error messages.
expected: The system either successfully removes the policy from Inventory, or displays an appropriate message indicating that policy removal must be done from Manage -> Policies section. If the latter, verify that the device is missing from that section, confirming the bug.
